top_stories.summary: THE chairman of the House Committee on Ways and Means warned that removing the value-added tax (VAT) on fuel could do more harm than good to the Philippine economy.

top_stories.title: ERC clarifies orders on bid of SMC units to recover cost
top_stories.summary: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) on Thursday clarified earlier rulings on how the units of San Miguel Global Power Holdings Corp. can recover approximately P29.21 billion in fuel costs and losses they incurred from 2022 to 2023 from the Manila Electric Co. (Meralco).

secondary_signals.title: Price Tracker: Oil, fuel monitor for Apr. 13
secondary_signals.summary: After weeks of increases, the government now expects a sharp fuel rollback, though officials say the oil shock is not over.

secondary_signals.title: FIRST FUEL PRICE ROLLBACK THIS YEAR | Gasoline down by P4.43/liter; diesel by P20.89
secondary_signals.summary: Fuel prices are set for sharp rollbacks this week, with reductions expected to reach at least P4.43 per liter for gasoline and as much as P20.89 per liter for diesel, the Department of Energy (DOE) said on Sunday. secondary_signals.title: Diesel price rollback seen at P20 per liter
secondary_signals.summary: MOTORISTS are finally getting a much-needed break after weeks of hefty increases, as the Department of Energy expects pump price rollbacks, with diesel prices seen dropping by at least P20 per liter (/l).

secondary_signals.title: Energy shocks drive higher coal use in region
secondary_signals.summary: SOUTHEAST ASIA is leaning more heavily on coal-fired power generation amid global oil shocks, with countries such as the Philippines seeing increased reliance, according to international research group Zero Carbon Analytics (ZCA). secondary_signals.title: Possible diesel rollback now estimated at P8.80-P10.80/liter
secondary_signals.summary: MANILA, Philippines – The potential diesel price cut next week may go up to as much as P10.80 a liter as the global market looks forward to the peace talks between the United States and Iran. secondary_signals.title: Bigger diesel price cut if Middle East truce holds
secondary_signals.summary: MANILA, Philippines — A cut in local diesel prices — about double what was initially expected — will provide a little relief to consumers next week as a ceasefire in the Middle East war continues to hold. secondary_signals.title: Levanta Renewables, CEEC seal EPC deal for solar power plant
secondary_signals.summary: Singapore-based Levanta Renewables has selected China Energy Engineering Group (CEEC) as the EPC (engineering, procurement and construction) contractor for the Barotac Solar Power and Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project in the Visayas region.
